title: Lantern: Local-First AI Chat for Families
content: Lantern is a privacy-first AI chat platform designed for families living in buses, vans, farms, and intentional communities. Works offline with Starlink. Zero cloud dependency. Kids can chat with Claude, Gemini, or DeepSeek. Parents control everything.

title: 5-Minute Quick Start
content: 1. Install Lantern from GitHub. 2. Run lantern-desktop-auth-ui.py. 3. Click Claude card. 4. Go to console.anthropic.com, get API key. 5. Paste key into Lantern. 6. Click Ready. Done! You're chatting with Claude.

title: Provider Authentication System
content: Lantern supports 5 LLM providers: Claude (API), Gemini (API), DeepSeek (API), LM Studio (local), Ollama (local). Credentials stored locally at ~/.lantern/credentials/ with owner-only permissions (0o600). Never synced to cloud. Never committed to git. Configuration at ~/.lantern/providers.json tracks primary, fallback, and family bindings.

title: Family Configuration
content: Lantern supports 3 family profiles: Family A (van, 2 kids 6-10yo, 2h session limit, 4h daily cap), Family B (bus, 3 kids 8-12yo, 2h session, 4h daily), Family C (farm, 1 kid 11-16yo, 3h session, 5h daily). Each family has age-appropriate parental controls, no cloud tracking, local-only storage. Parents see all child queries (opt-in).

title: Installation Guide
content: Step 1: Clone repo from GitHub. Step 2: Install Python 3.9+ and Tkinter. Step 3: Run: python scripts/lantern-desktop-auth-ui.py. Step 4: Click provider card (Claude recommended). Step 5: Get API key from provider console. Step 6: Paste into Lantern form. Step 7: Click Save. Step 8: Set as Primary. Step 9: Click Ready. Troubleshooting: If button not responsive, press Tab to navigate keyboard-only.

title: Accessibility Features
content: Lantern is built for users with disabilities. Keyboard-only navigation (Tab, Shift+Tab, Enter). High contrast (white #FFFFFF on black #000000, 21:1 ratio, WCAG AAA). Large fonts (18pt minimum). Screen reader support (all buttons labeled). Audio narration (12 audio files, one per step). Motor accessibility (buttons 200x50px, 44x44px WCAG minimum). No mouse required. No audio-only content (text alternative always). Plain language (no jargon).

title: Step 1: Open Lantern Desktop
content: Press Windows Key. Type: lantern. Press Enter. The Lantern authentication window opens. You see 5 provider cards: Claude (brain icon), Gemini (palette), DeepSeek (lightning), LM Studio (computer), Ollama (llama). Audio: 'Press Windows Key, type lantern, press Enter. The Lantern window opens with provider cards.'

title: Step 2: Select Claude
content: Press Tab until blue border appears around Claude card. Press Enter to select. Form opens showing 'Configure Anthropic Claude' with instructions: Get API key from https://console.anthropic.com/. Audio: 'Press Tab to move focus. When Claude is highlighted, press Enter. The Claude setup form appears.'

title: Step 3: Get Claude API Key
content: Open browser. Go to console.anthropic.com. Sign in with Google. Click 'API Keys' (left menu). Click 'Create Key'. Copy long code starting with 'sk-ant-'. Return to Lantern. Press Tab to API Key field. Paste: Ctrl+V. Key shows as dots (•••) for security. Audio: 'Open your browser. Go to console-dot-anthropic-dot-com. Sign in. Click API Keys. Click Create Key. Copy the code. Come back to Lantern. Paste.'

title: Step 4: Save Credentials
content: Press Tab to 'Save Provider Credentials' button. Press Enter. Success message appears: 'Anthropic Claude configured! Credentials saved securely.' File stored at: ~/.lantern/credentials/claude.json (owner-only, 0o600 permissions). Credentials never synced to cloud, never committed to git. Audio: 'Tab to Save button. Press Enter. Green checkmark appears. Claude is now configured.'

title: Step 5: Set Primary Provider
content: Back at main screen. Claude now shows checkmark (✅) instead of red circle (⭕). Press Tab to 'Set Primary Provider' button. Press Enter. Select claude. Press Enter. Message: 'Primary provider set to: claude'. This tells Lantern to always use Claude first when chatting. Audio: 'Tab to Set Primary Provider. Press Enter. Select Claude. Press Enter. Primary provider is now set.'

title: Step 6: Click Ready
content: Press Tab until blue border is on [✅ Ready] button (bottom right). Press Enter. Authentication window closes. Lantern Chat window opens with text box ready for input. You are now in Lantern Chat! Type a question: 'What is the capital of France?' Press Ctrl+Enter to send. Claude responds. Audio: 'Tab to the Ready button. Press Enter. The chat window opens. You are now ready to talk with Claude.'

title: QA Test Results
content: All tests PASSED. UI Launch: ✅. Tab Navigation: ✅. DeepSeek Clickable: ✅ (BUG FIXED). Form Opens: ✅. Keyboard-Only: ✅. HTML Tutorial: ✅. Audio System: ✅. Button Sizing: ✅ (exceeds WCAG AA minimum). High Contrast: ✅ (21:1 ratio). Focus Indicators: ✅ (blue border visible). No crashes. Graceful fallback working. Status: PRODUCTION READY.

title: Claude Provider Setup
content: Provider: Anthropic Claude. Type: API Key. Docs: https://console.anthropic.com/. Setup: 1. Create account at console.anthropic.com. 2. Click API Keys. 3. Click Create Key. 4. Copy key (starts with sk-ant-). 5. Enter in Lantern. Storage: ~/.lantern/credentials/claude.json. Permissions: 0o600 (owner-only). Cost: Variable (per-token pricing, starts free). Status: ACTIVE

title: Gemini Provider Setup
content: Provider: Google Gemini. Type: API Key. Docs: https://makersuite.google.com/. Setup: 1. Go to makersuite.google.com. 2. Sign in with Google. 3. Click Create API Key. 4. Select project. 5. Copy key. 6. Enter in Lantern. Storage: ~/.lantern/credentials/gemini.json. Permissions: 0o600. Cost: Free tier available. Recommended as FALLBACK provider.

title: DeepSeek Provider Setup
content: Provider: DeepSeek. Type: API Key. Docs: https://platform.deepseek.com/. Setup: 1. Go to platform.deepseek.com. 2. Sign up or sign in. 3. Navigate to API Keys. 4. Create new key. 5. Copy when shown. 6. Enter in Lantern. Storage: ~/.lantern/credentials/deepseek.json. Permissions: 0o600. Cost: Check pricing at platform.deepseek.com. Status: SUPPORTED

title: LM Studio Provider Setup
content: Provider: Local LM Studio. Type: Endpoint (localhost:1234). Docs: https://lmstudio.ai/. Setup: 1. Download LM Studio from lmstudio.ai. 2. Install locally. 3. Download a model (e.g., Qwen, Llama). 4. Launch inference server (default: localhost:1234). 5. In Lantern, select LM Studio. 6. Enter Host: 127.0.0.1, Port: 1234. 7. Save. Cost: FREE (runs on your PC). Status: WORKS OFFLINE, NO API KEY NEEDED

title: Ollama Provider Setup
content: Provider: Ollama. Type: Endpoint (localhost:11434). Docs: https://ollama.ai/. Setup: 1. Download Ollama from ollama.ai. 2. Install locally. 3. Run: ollama pull llama2 (or another model). 4. Ollama server runs on localhost:11434. 5. In Lantern, select Ollama. 6. Enter Host: 127.0.0.1, Port: 11434. 7. Save. Cost: FREE (runs on your PC). Status: FASTEST LOCAL OPTION

title: Foundry Resource Pool
content: 1 Founder + 20 Operators × 20 PCs = 40 effective units. Each operator can grant (opt-in): GPU compute (idle hours), SSD/HDD storage (encrypted shards), RAM (off-hours), network bandwidth, API quota (routed through Suzie when operator idle), agent slot capacity, public IP/port forward. Hard boundaries: NEVER pool personal files, browser data, passwords, webcam, microphone (except Lantern Kids with parental consent), anything outside foundry workspace. Consent stored in ~/.foundry/consent.json. Default: ALL OFF (operator must explicitly enable each resource). Withdrawal: Single config edit, honored within 60 seconds.

title: Revenue Model Year 1
content: Lantern Kids: 10 families × $20/mo = $200/mo = $2,400/yr. Foundry secondary revenue (operator resource pool): 5% of foundry work = ~$500/yr. Total Year 1: $2,900 conservative estimate. Year 2 target: $4M+ (20 families + scaled foundry + Suzie SaaS). Success metric: 10 families, 98%+ retention, unsolicited referrals.

title: 4-Week Go-to-Market Strategy
content: Week 1: Deploy installer, recruit Family A, send 10 outreach messages. Week 2: Family A feedback, start parental controls, recruit Family B. Week 3: Family A → FIRST PAYING CUSTOMER, Suzie orchestration design. Week 4: 3 families active (1 paying), foundry tested. Success metric: $200/mo MRR by Week 4. Lead: Founder (warm outreach to network). Execution: 30-day founder-led sales cycle, no cold outreach.
